title: Week-4 Eval Dashboard

provenance.dataset_version_note: Dataset version 2026-06-24-plan1 (frozen): baseline and all three current-main runs were evaluated against this same 40-case golden set — the dataset did not change between baseline and re-evaluation, so the deltas are valid. Composition: 16 happy / 9 edge / 5 known_failure / 10 adversarial.
provenance.redaction_policy: Committed dashboard data contains aggregate metrics, synthetic case IDs, and public-safe caveats only; no raw learner text, tutor prose, retrieved spans, raw traces, private URLs, secrets, or frozen test data.

hero.primary_question: Did the Week-4 improvements measurably help without breaking grounded refusal safety?
hero.verdict: Citation F1 improved by +0.150 and turn p95 latency improved by 27%; retrieval recall and refusal recall held at 1.000. Tradeoffs: refusal precision fell from 0.833 to 0.791 and task completion moved from 94.7% infra-excluded baseline to 93.3% current mean; citation F1 clears the 0.50 short-term floor but remains about 0.31 below the 0.90 plan pass bar.

kpis.label: Citation F1
kpis.note: Cleared the 0.50 short-term floor, but still below the 0.90 plan pass bar.

kpis.label: Turn p95 latency
kpis.note: The clearest speed win from loop controls and attribution work.

kpis.label: Refusal recall
kpis.note: The never-bluff guardrail stayed intact across all three runs.

kpis.label: Retrieval recall@5
kpis.note: Retrieval stayed healthy; failures are mostly post-retrieval behavior.

kpis.label: Task completion
kpis.note: Baseline excludes two infra errors; current mean includes all 40 cases.

kpis.label: Refusal precision
kpis.note: Small-N precision moved with a few teachable false refusals.

metric_deltas.note: Baseline excluded two infra errors; current runs had no infra exclusions.
Remaining misses are concentrated false refusals.
Cleared the 0.50 short-term floor, but remains below the 0.90 plan pass bar.
Tiny-N metric; a few false refusals move it visibly.
The core grounding safety guardrail held.
Slight movement; not the main story.
Retrieval was not the primary remaining bottleneck.
Token usage stayed in the same band.
Baseline and run 1 pricing env vars were unset; use runs 2 and 3 only as current cost reference.

improvement_levers.change: Single structured-output retry on missing/invalid agent output; tokens accumulated.
improvement_levers.measured: No infra errors across three runs; happy_014 now a visible, countable refusal.

improvement_levers.change: Correct deterministic grade plus grounded evidence forces advance.
improvement_levers.cluster: Correct answers that did not advance
improvement_levers.predicted_impact: Better task flow; refusal recall must stay 1.000
improvement_levers.measured: Task flow held; refusal recall stayed 1.000.

improvement_levers.change: Preferred check-span signal: slide, then handout, then first citeable span.
improvement_levers.cluster: Citation mismatch (dominant cluster; 16 teachable rows at citation F1=0)
improvement_levers.predicted_impact: Hold or improve citation F1 above the 0.50 floor

improvement_levers.change: Cap/reuse repeated retrieval, check generation, and escalation within a turn.
improvement_levers.cluster: Latency tail and repeated tool loops
improvement_levers.predicted_impact: Turn p95 -1 to -2s; tokens flat or down

improvement_levers.change: Broad citation fallback to anchor final answers.

production_monitoring.threshold: Alert if either drops > 10% vs trailing baseline
production_monitoring.rationale: Catches retrieval/citation regressions before learners feel them.
Usually signals a tool-loop or retrieval blow-up.

production_monitoring.threshold: Alert if > 10s SLA on > 5% of runs

production_monitoring.rationale: Over-conservative refusal is our dominant failure mode — watch it live.

production_monitoring.metric: Per-tool error rate: retrieve / generate_check / grade (1h)
production_monitoring.rationale: Flags external-dependency outage or structured-output regression.

open_axial.open_code: Expected span retrieved but scored ~0.31, below the 0.40 STOP threshold; agent refuses rather than teach from weak evidence.
Expected span present but scored 0.0 on the resolved query (query drift); was an infra error at baseline, now a stable refusal.
open_axial.axial_code: Query drift / possible label issue -> over-conservative refusal

open_axial.open_code: Intermittent false refusal (2 of 3 runs); citeable evidence sits at the threshold.
Agent built the check from a weaker note/transcript span; final answer faithfully cites it but mismatches the golden citation.

guardrails.label: Refusal recall
guardrails.note: All refusal-expected cases still refused instead of bluffing.

guardrails.label: Retrieval recall@5
guardrails.note: Expected spans remained retrievable in the top five.

guardrails.label: Scorer integrity
guardrails.note: Dashboard work does not alter eval scoring or denominators.

guardrails.label: Privacy
guardrails.note: Committed artifact uses aggregate metrics and synthetic case labels only.

remaining_failures.summary: Stable false refusal across all three current-main runs.
Stable false refusal; do not lower STOP threshold casually.
False refusal in two of three current-main runs.
No current infra failure, but kept on watchlist from historical instability.

perspectives.user: The tutor is faster and cites better, and it still refuses unsupported questions. The visible product risk is over-refusing a few teachable cases.
perspectives.builder: Retrieval is healthy; remaining work is mostly post-retrieval decision behavior, false-refusal calibration, and citation quality rather than index recall.

notes: Current values are the mean of three full current-main runs.
Citation improved materially and clears the 0.50 short-term floor, but remains below the original 0.90 aspirational pass bar.
Cost delta vs baseline is not meaningful because baseline and run 1 pricing env vars were unset.
Case IDs are synthetic scenario labels and carry no learner text or corpus text.
Private project and dataset URLs belong in local-only notes or submission materials, not in this committed dashboard.
